prompt_variant: You are a critical academic research verifier. Your task: determine if a bibliog...
_search_results:
This paper **exists** and is a research work on reducing cognitive biases in large language models through a "hashing" technique.[1]

**Exact Title:** "Meaningless is better: hashing bias-inducing words in LLM prompts improves performance in logical reasoning and statistical learning"[1]

**Authors:** Milena Chadimová, Eduard Jurášek, Tomáš Kliegr[1]

**Venue:** arXiv (Computer Science - Computation and Language)[1]

**Year:** 2024 (submitted November 26, 2024; last revised June 11, 2025)[1]

**arXiv Identifier:** 2411.17304v2[1]

**DOI/URL:** Available at https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.17304[1]

**Institutional Affiliation:** Prague University of Economics and Business[3]

The paper introduces a novel method called "hashing" that masks potentially bias-inducing words in LLM prompts with meaningless hash-like identifiers to reduce cognitive biases and reliance on external knowledge. The research was tested across three sets of experiments involving 490 prompts across multiple models including Llama, ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini, and Mixtral, with results analyzed using chi-square statistical tests.[1]
Semantic Scholar: no matching paper found.

**Yes, the paper exists.**

- **Exact title**: Meaningless is better: hashing bias-inducing words in LLM prompts improves performance in logical reasoning and statistical learning[3].
- **Authors**: Milena Chadimová, Eduard Jurášek, Tomáš Kliegr[1][3][4].
- **Venue**: arXiv (preprint)[1][3].
- **arXiv ID**: arXiv:2411.17304 (version 2 as of 11 Jun 2025)[3].
- **Submission history**: v1 submitted 26 Nov 2024; v2 last revised 11 Jun 2025[3].
- **Subjects**: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)[3].
- **DOI**: https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2411.17304[3].

The paper introduces a "hashing" method that masks potentially bias-inducing words in LLM prompts to improve logical reasoning and statistical learning performance[3]. It is affiliated with researchers from Prague, Czechia[1].

The paper **does exist** on arXiv. Here are the details:

**Title:** Meaningless is better: hashing bias-inducing words in LLM prompts improves performance in logical reasoning and statistical learning[6]

**Authors:** Milena Chadimová, Eduard Jurášek, Tomáš Kliegr[6]

**Venue:** arXiv (Computer Science - Computation and Language)[6]

**Year:** 2024 (submitted November 26, 2024; revised June 11, 2025)[6]

**arXiv ID:** 2411.17304[6]

**DOI:** https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2411.17304[6]

The paper introduces a "hashing" method that masks bias-inducing words in large language models with meaningless identifiers to reduce cognitive biases[6]. It was tested across 490 prompts in three experimental sets[6].

Regarding the Semantic Scholar status: The search results provided do not indicate whether this specific paper is currently indexed in Semantic Scholar's database. The results show that Semantic Scholar indexes over 233 million papers[4], but there is no explicit confirmation either way for arXiv:2411.17304.
